article_title: KubeVirt 1.8 Introduces Hypervisor Abstraction Layer, Breaking KVM Lock-In for Kubernetes Virtual Machines

content_preview.title: KubeVirt 1.8 Introduces Hypervisor Abstraction Layer, Breaking KVM Lock-In for Kubernetes Virtual Machines
content_preview.summary: The CNCF incubating project's latest release adds a multi-hypervisor backend, Intel TDX attestation for confidential computing, and PCIe NUMA topology awareness for AI workloads, while scaling its test framework to 8,000 virtual machines.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The KubeVirt project released version 1.8 on March 25 at KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Europe 2026 in Amsterdam, delivering what the community describes as its most architecturally significant update since the project joined the Cloud Native Computing Foundation in 2022. The headline feature is a Hypervisor Abstraction Layer that decouples KubeVirt from its longstanding dependency on KVM, opening the door to alternative hypervisor backends while [preserving KVM as the default](https://ww...

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent DevOps article. InfoQ/CNCF blog verified.
